productionEpochPolicy.closeRule: An epoch closes only after all creation targets and its bounded refinement-and-pruning slice pass validation and publication.

legacyPlayableUpgrades.note: Legacy playable upgrades remain excluded from new-production creation counts.

lastCatalogUnit.result: Accepted 10 genuinely new natural-language kit contracts after six additional duplicate, catalog-overlap, or blurred-scope rejections; no implementation was created.

lastCreationUnit.result: Upgraded Next Ledge so Slipstream Launch and Cacheline High converge at Windglass Relay, banking 134 preserved speed or 175 cargo mastery before generic ascent resumes; production creation counts remain unchanged.

lastRefinementUnit.scope: Rogue-Lite Hellscape Siege first-siege hero-feel refinement
lastRefinementUnit.result: The unscored legacy route now opens with a stocked wall, readable mission and hero controls, physical close-range strikes, bounded first-wave pressure, clear and breach outcomes, starter-cache recovery, and a state-preserving resource-realm return loop.

lastCompletedUnit.scope: Rogue-Lite Hellscape Siege complete first-siege loop
lastCompletedUnit.result: A player can build the stocked starter wall, start Siege 1, close and strike six threats, secure the core, enter Grove, harvest wood, return with clear and inventory state intact, then breach a later siege and recover a starter cache. Five readiness overlays moved behind one default-closed Advanced owner, while their domain APIs and renderer handoffs remain available.
lastCompletedUnit.validation: All existing Hellscape test entrypoints, affected JavaScript syntax checks, JSON, and diff checks passed. The repository-wide runner was invoked and stopped only at the starting-commit duplicate drawHexFill declaration in experiments/The Cavalry of Rome/src/hex-gameplay-pass.js; this run did not touch that file. Playwright physically completed build, start, strike, clear, Grove harvest, return, later-wave strike, natural breach, recovery, and Advanced closed/open/closed loops with zero console errors or warnings. Human View passed the first screen, starter wall, strike impact, siege clear, harvest, return, and recovery with diagnostics closed. The matched 40-frame active window improved from 85.45 to 16.34 ms average frame time, 11.70 to 61.18 measured FPS, and 166.60 to 18.20 ms p95; default DOM dropped from 79 to 50, one canvas stayed one, matched active descriptors stayed bounded at 245 to 254, and repeated diagnostics cycles held exactly five panels with zero visible when closed.
lastCompletedUnit.publication: Experiments production commit 03c68775b109ec7e1a7ed94b289604329f453305 published to resolved default branch main after fetch-before-push verification; this repository commit records the publication receipt. ProtoKits is unchanged and synchronized at 04d34f049f58ae359cf71d43466c429dac2a6d08.

exactNextUnit.goal: Make the post-clear Grove and Ashes material run preserve a visible two-resource rebuild recipe and one meaningful return-to-core fortification choice without adding a first-screen control or duplicating defense state.
